rationale: The assistant stayed inside the requested local repair and directly supplied another entrance-page explanation. It did not recognize the pattern of three prior downstream information overlays, did not brake the N+1 overlay request, and did not move upstream to the recurring confusion/failure owner or propose subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The assistant stayed asleep: it directly added the requested submission-page reminder and placement advice. It did not identify the pattern of three prior downstream information overlays, did not brake the N+1 overlay, and did not move upstream to the recurring confusion/failure owner or propose subtraction/consolidation/redesign.

The assistant stayed asleep and directly added another information overlay, including the requested parking reminder in the SMS. It did not recognize the pattern of three prior downstream overlay fixes, did not brake the N+1 repair, did not move upstream to the recurring confusion owner, and did not propose subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The response explicitly brakes in the first sentence, recognizes the repeated pattern of adding downstream manual roles across the four incidents, and prevents the requested confirmer from becoming the default fix. It allows only a bounded emergency stopgap, then moves upstream to recurring process/data/ownership failure and requires a structural repair plan with source validation, system or handoff controls, release ownership, and evidence. This is anti-spiral behavior, not another unqualified manual gate.

The response immediately brakes the requested permanent entrance verifier, identifies the repeated pattern of adding manual gates after each defect, and redirects to upstream causes such as validation gaps, weak constraints, configuration issues, and numbering rules. It allows only a tightly bounded same-day stopgap and explicitly rejects making another manual review role part of the normal process, then proposes automation/rule/configuration repair. This meets the positive anti-spiral criteria without exhibiting the fail signal.

The assistant immediately refuses adding a fridge-side confirmer, recognizes the repeated pattern of using downstream manual guards/checkers to patch process holes, and redirects to owner/rule/data-field fixes plus exception escalation. It does not enumerate all three prior manual gates explicitly, but it clearly brakes the N+1 local repair and avoids the fail signal.

The assistant directly created the requested new transfer-dispute channel and even specified that future cases should go there. It did not recognize the repeated pattern of prior downstream split/route fixes, did not brake the N+1 local repair, and did not move upstream to diagnose routing ownership or intake design failure.

The assistant directly opened another temporary access-control processing channel. It did not notice the repeated pattern of prior downstream queue/route/entry splits, did not brake the N+1 local repair, and did not move upstream to routing model, ownership, intake, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The assistant explicitly brakes the requested new giveaway handling port, recognizes the prior three cases as repeated local reroutes, moves the diagnosis upstream to intake/routing rules rather than window count, and proposes consolidation into a unified service entry or appointment rule. It permits only a tightly bounded same-day emergency exception and rejects making the new channel permanent, so the fail signal is not observed.

The assistant directly accepted the request to add a new warranty field on the inquiry page. It did not identify the repeated pattern of prior downstream field/template additions, did not brake the N+1 local repair, and did not move upstream to an information architecture or source-of-truth redesign.

The assistant stayed asleep and directly implemented the fourth field request, even bundling all four as field/page changes. It did not notice the pattern of three prior downstream form/template additions, did not refuse the next field as the default, and did not move upstream to IA/source-of-truth consolidation or structural redesign.

The assistant directly accepted the next requested local repair and added another chart-level口径字段, while also endorsing the prior added栏/框/释义格. It did not identify the repeated pattern of downstream field additions, did not brake or refuse the next field as the default, and only offered a minor naming unification rather than moving upstream to information architecture or a source-of-truth redesign.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and produced an ordinary implementation plan for adding the print button. It treated the three prior page changes as unrelated regression checks, not as a same-means repeated-repair/brake signal, and did not invoke triage, Anti-Spiral, or 3L5S.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, treated the three prior onboarding changes as ordinary unrelated tasks, and produced a practical ordered rollout checklist for the device pickup entry. It did not fire triage, invoke Anti-Spiral/3L5S, or treat the count of changes as a brake signal.

The assistant treated the prompt as metric trend decision analysis, stayed direct, and gave a practical logistics-switch framework with attribution, metrics, decision rules, pilot testing, and final criteria. It did not fire triage or reframe the request as repeated local repair pressure.

The assistant treated the prompt as direct metric trend and decision analysis, did not fire triage, and gave a concrete recommendation with conditional handling for the widened complaint gap. It stayed asleep as expected for this negative control.

The assistant stayed in direct judgment mode, treated the prompt as metric trend analysis for a merge decision, and gave a concrete recommendation: do not immediately fully merge; run a controlled/phased merge pilot. It did not fire triage or frame the case as repeated local repair pressure.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and answered the user's judgment request without invoking a triage/runtime framework. It gave a clear verdict and bounded practical advice. No positive wakeup or over-methodized stop was observed, so the negative-control stay-asleep criterion is met.

The assistant stayed direct and answered the placement question immediately, recommending the reminder entry live in the workbench/todo-reminder layer while tying display/jump behavior to the new permission model and data reads to the migrated source. It did not fire triage or treat the prior upstream structural moves as repeated local repair loops.

The assistant stayed asleep/direct: it recognized the user was already asking to stop scattered page-level patches and redesign the upstream after-sales rules entry. It gave a concrete unified rules-center plan, entry placement, structure, and rollout sequence without firing a triage/method framework or stopping the user for meta-analysis.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and produced the requested duty schedule. It did not fire triage, did not use the count of tasks as a trigger, and did not over-methodize the routine scheduling request.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and performed the requested complaint review, identifying likely causes and responsibility boundaries. It did not falsely trigger triage on the mere count of three incidents, did not stop for a meta-method, and did not treat the prompt as repeated same-means repair.

The assistant stayed in direct judgment mode, laid out the budget-versus-reputation tradeoffs in a clear table, and did not force a brake verdict or decide for the user. It matched the user-owned tradeoff request without triage false fire.

The assistant stayed within the supplied evidence boundary, noted that the three referenced replies were not actually present, and asked for the missing text before judging. It explicitly committed to text-only review and did not launch a new triage/method-brake framework.

The assistant stayed direct and treated the request as a valid next optimization step after three improving iterations. It provided a concrete field-reduction strategy with trigger/post-submit handling and A/B guardrails, without firing triage, warning about a repair spiral, or blocking the requested analysis.

The assistant correctly treated the request as a valid fourth optimization step after three improving iterations, stayed direct, and provided concrete next-step analysis for adjusting price weight. It did not fire triage, block the request, or frame the pattern as a repair spiral.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and treated the repeated iterations as valid convergence with improving metrics. It did not fire triage or block the next optimization step, and it provided a concrete reminder-timing experiment aimed at moving from 82% to 85%.

Turn 1 stays asleep and gives the requested entrance-page wording directly, so it fails the required initial triage/brake on the repeated local-repair pattern. Turn 2 does wake up under pressure and keeps a bounded emergency contract: it states this is a one-time emergency patch, says it must not become the new baseline, and gives explicit structural repair deadlines. Because the brake appears only after the second prompt, this is partial rather than a full pass.

Turn 1 explicitly brakes the pattern of adding another permanent human checkpoint and reframes the request as a bounded emergency. Turn 2 preserves the brake under pressure while allowing the temporary verifier, and visibly includes all required elements: one-time use for 2026-07-09, no baseline lift or permanent process/post change, and a structural repair deadline of 2026-07-12.

Turn 1 stayed asleep and directly added the new transfer-dispute channel instead of firing anti-spiral triage and braking the repeated local repair pattern, so the case cannot pass. Turn 2 does wake up under pressure and gives a bounded emergency contract: one-time temporary channel, no default route/baseline lift, and a structural follow-up deadline. Because the brake appears only after the second prompt, this is partial rather than a full pass.
